description_long: This is a two-story school building with a prominent, sloped red-tiled roof and a rectangular footprint. The exterior walls are painted white and feature large, grid-like windows on both floors, allowing ample natural light into the interior. The main entrance is accentuated by a small overhang and a set of steps, visible on one side of the building. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete or masonry, with glass window panels and metal framing. The roof design includes a central raised section, adding architectural interest and likely providing additional headroom or ventilation. The overall appearance is functional and institutional, typical of mid-20th-century educational architecture.
description: A two-story white school building with a red-tiled sloped roof, large grid windows, and a prominent main entrance. The design is functional and institutional, with a rectangular layout and raised central roof section.
description_view_0: This view shows the side and rear of the school, highlighting the red-tiled roof, white walls, and multiple large windows.
description_view_1: This view presents the main entrance with steps and overhang, as well as the full expanse of windows along the front facade.
description_view_2: This angle displays the opposite side and rear, emphasizing the symmetry of the roof and window placement.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the back of the building, showing a continuous row of windows and the simple, unadorned rear facade.
